title: Truist Financial And U.S. Bancorp: Cause For Concern After The Stress Test?
text: Truist and U.S. Bancorp were among the weaker banks according to the 2023 Federal Reserve's stress test results, at least at first glance. The article discusses the parameters of the most severe scenario, evaluates the test results for Truist and U.S. Bancorp, and touches on the results for other banks (e.g., Deutsche, Capital One). I explain why the "severely adverse scenario" deserves its name and why investors should not dismiss the results as meaningless just because they do not include a nationwide bank run.

title: Truist announces planned dividend and stress capital buffer requirement following release of 2023 CCAR results
text: CHARLOTTE, N.C. , June 30, 2023 /PRNewswire/ -- Truist Financial Corporation (NYSE: TFC) today announced the Federal Reserve Board has determined the Company's preliminary stress capital buffer ("SCB") requirement is 2.9%.

title: 51 Ideal July Value Buys From Ben Graham
text: “Value ranking looks at the price of a stock relative to intrinsic firm value. Graham Value Stocks match the criteria Benjamin Graham followed.“—YCharts Value Screener. Here are 64 July value large caps and Graham formula-derived results that reflect established value-stock detection-criteria. Of those, 51 met the dogcatcher-ideal of dividends from $1K invested exceeding single-share-stock-prices. Representing nine-of-eleven Morningstar Sectors, all 51 ideal value-picks pay regular-dividends. Broker-target-top-ten net-gains ranged 38.26%-104.37%, topped by SBSW & PDM, as-of 6/23/23.

title: Beat the Dow Jones With This Cash-Gushing Dividend Stock
text: Truist has seen its share price decline along with most super-regional bank stocks this year. The bank now offers close to a 7% dividend yield and potentially good long-term returns on the stock as well.

title: ‘Cautious' JPMorgan analyst cuts price targets on nine major banks
text: J.P. Morgan analyst Vivek Juneja on Tuesday cut price targets on nine major U.S. banks due to his “cautious” view on the sector in the face of increased regulatory requirements, declining net interest income and higher expenses pressured by inflation. The comment came a day ahead of the Fed's stress tests results on bank capital.

title: Truist announces second-quarter 2023 earnings call details
text: CHARLOTTE, N.C. , June 20, 2023 /PRNewswire/ -- Truist Financial Corporation (NYSE: TFC) will report second-quarter 2023 financial results before the market opens on Thursday, July 20, 2023.

title: David Smith named Head of Truist Mortgage
text: Smith succeeds Todd Chamberlain, who will retire CHARLOTTE, N.C. , June 20, 2023 /PRNewswire/ -- Truist Financial Corporation (NYSE: TFC) today announced that David Smith has been named head of Truist Mortgage.

title: Regional Banks Have Begun to Adjust Their Second-Quarter Guidance
text: Higher funding costs are putting pressure on net interest income and margins. Several management teams seem to have underestimated just how tough conditions might become.

title: Truist Foundation grants LIFT $500,000 to expand its services to Richmond to support underserved families
text: LIFT will partner with Richmond's Office of Community Wealth Building to create one-on-one coaching model to support self-sufficiency for families within community RICHMOND, Va. , June 9, 2023 /PRNewswire/ -- Truist Foundation today announced a $500,000 grant to LIFT, a nonprofit organization dedicated to advancing economic equity by implementing holistic, human-focused social service systems within marginalized communities.

title: Is Truist Financial (TFC) Worth a Watch on 6.3% Dividend Yield?
text: Amid the turmoil in the banking industry and recessionary fears, investors can watch the Truist Financial (TFC) stock for its solid dividend yield.

title: My Top 10 High Yield Dividend Stocks For June 2023
text: I present my top 10 High Yield dividend stocks that are poised to offer strong future returns. The top 10 stocks on the watchlist offer a collective 5.08% dividend yield and have grown their dividends at a historical rate of 35.55% per year over the last five years. New stocks on the watchlist include Best Buy, Goldman Sachs, and Paychex, all of which appear undervalued based on dividend yield theory and have strong dividend growth histories.

title: Zelle and Chase working to resolve issue with accidental double payments
text: A spokesperson for JPMorgan Chase & Co. JPM, +2.40% on Friday has confirmed statements on social media that some customers that use Zelle are seeing duplicate transactions and fees on their checking accounts. “We're sorry,” the spokesperson said in an email to MarketWatch.
